11	 * ```

13	 * @module pipelines
14	 */

1956	/**
1957	 * Image To Text pipeline using a `AutoModelForVision2Seq`. This pipeline predicts a caption for a given image.
1958	 *
1959	 * **Example:** Generate a caption for an image w/ `Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning`.
1960	 * ```javascript
1961	 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning');
1962	 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
1963	 * const output = await captioner(url);
1964	 * // [{ generated_text: 'a cat laying on a couch with another cat' }]
1965	 * ```
1966	 *
1967	 * **Example:** Optical Character Recognition (OCR) w/ `Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten`.
1968	 * ```javascript
1969	 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten');
1970	 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/handwriting.jpg';
1971	 * const output = await captioner(url);
1972	 * // [{ generated_text: 'Mr. Brown commented icily.' }]
1973	 * ```
1974	 */
1975	export class ImageToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
1976	
1977	    /**
1978	     * Create a new ImageToTextPipeline.
1979	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
1980	     */
1981	    constructor(options) {
1982	        super(options);
1983	    }
1984	
1985	    /** @type {ImageToTextPipelineCallback} */
1986	    async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {}) {
1987	
1988	        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
1989	        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);
1990	
1991	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);
1992	
1993	        const toReturn = [];
1994	        for (const batch of pixel_values) {
1995	            batch.dims = [1, ...batch.dims]
1996	            const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: batch, ...generate_kwargs });
1997	            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
1998	                skip_special_tokens: true,
1999	            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }))
2000	            toReturn.push(decoded);
2001	        }
2002	
2003	        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
2004	    }
2005	}

3410	 * @typedef {{[K in AliasType]: InstanceType<typeof SUPPORTED_TASKS[TASK_ALIASES[K]]["pipeline"]>}} AliasTasks A mapping from pipeline aliases to their corresponding pipeline classes.

3413	
3414	/**
3415	 * Utility factory method to build a `Pipeline` object.
3416	 *
3417	 * @template {PipelineType} T The type of pipeline to return.
3418	 * @param {T} task The task defining which pipeline will be returned. Currently accepted tasks are:
3419	 *  - `"audio-classification"`: will return a `AudioClassificationPipeline`.
3420	 *  - `"automatic-speech-recognition"`: will return a `AutomaticSpeechRecognitionPipeline`.
3421	 *  - `"depth-estimation"`: will return a `DepthEstimationPipeline`.
3422	 *  - `"document-question-answering"`: will return a `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline`.
3423	 *  - `"feature-extraction"`: will return a `FeatureExtractionPipeline`.
3424	 *  - `"fill-mask"`: will return a `FillMaskPipeline`.
3425	 *  - `"image-classification"`: will return a `ImageClassificationPipeline`.
3426	 *  - `"image-segmentation"`: will return a `ImageSegmentationPipeline`.
3427	 *  - `"image-to-text"`: will return a `ImageToTextPipeline`.
3428	 *  - `"object-detection"`: will return a `ObjectDetectionPipeline`.
3429	 *  - `"question-answering"`: will return a `QuestionAnsweringPipeline`.
3430	 *  - `"summarization"`: will return a `SummarizationPipeline`.
3431	 *  - `"text2text-generation"`: will return a `Text2TextGenerationPipeline`.
3432	 *  - `"text-classification"` (alias "sentiment-analysis" available): will return a `TextClassificationPipeline`.
3433	 *  - `"text-generation"`: will return a `TextGenerationPipeline`.
3434	 *  - `"token-classification"` (alias "ner" available): will return a `TokenClassificationPipeline`.
3435	 *  - `"translation"`: will return a `TranslationPipeline`.
3436	 *  - `"translation_xx_to_yy"`: will return a `TranslationPipeline`.
3437	 *  - `"zero-shot-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotClassificationPipeline`.
3438	 *  - `"zero-shot-audio-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotAudioClassificationPipeline`.
3439	 *  - `"zero-shot-image-classification"`: will return a `ZeroShotImageClassificationPipeline`.
3440	 *  - `"zero-shot-object-detection"`: will return a `ZeroShotObjectDetectionPipeline`.
3441	 * @param {string} [model=null] The name of the pre-trained model to use. If not specified, the default model for the task will be used.
3442	 * @param {import('./utils/hub.js').PretrainedModelOptions} [options] Optional parameters for the pipeline.
3443	 * @returns {Promise<AllTasks[T]>} A Pipeline object for the specified task.
3444	 * @throws {Error} If an unsupported pipeline is requested.
3445	 */

1960	 * ```javascript

1962	 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
1963	 * const output = await captioner(url);

1965	 * ```
1966	 *

1968	 * ```javascript

1975	export class ImageToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
1976	
1977	    /**
1978	     * Create a new ImageToTextPipeline.
1979	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
1980	     */
1981	    constructor(options) {
1982	        super(options);
1983	    }
1984	
1985	    /** @type {ImageToTextPipelineCallback} */
1986	    async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {}) {
1987	
1988	        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
1989	        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);
1990	
1991	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);
1992	
1993	        const toReturn = [];
1994	        for (const batch of pixel_values) {
1995	            batch.dims = [1, ...batch.dims]
1996	            const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: batch, ...generate_kwargs });
1997	            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
1998	                skip_special_tokens: true,
1999	            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }))
2000	            toReturn.push(decoded);
2001	        }
2002	
2003	        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
2004	    }
2005	}

Now I understand the `_prepare_model_inputs` logic:

1. It does `const model_inputs = pick(model_kwargs, this.forward_params)` - picks only the keys declared in `forward_params` from kwargs
2. If `model_inputs` already has the `main_input_name` (which is `input_ids` for LLaVA), it uses that
3. Otherwise, it uses the `inputs` parameter

So for LLaVA with `forward_params = ['input_ids', 'attention_mask', 'pixel_values', 'position_ids', 'past_key_values']`:


When we spread `{ input_ids, attention_mask, pixel_values }` into `generate()`, the `_prepare_model_inputs` method picks those keys from the kwargs and finds `input_ids` already present, so it uses that directly without needing the fallback `inputs` parameter. This confirms the pattern at line 784 works as intended.

For the image-text-to-text pipeline, I need to think through batching. Single inputs return a flat array with one result, but the processor might handle batching differently depending on image sizes. Let me keep the implementation straightforward for now and follow what the processor naturally supports. I'm realizing the processor handles batching natively—it can take arrays of images and text strings, returning combined tensors for the whole batch. The model's generate function then outputs a tensor with one sequence per batch element, and the tokenizer's batch_decode converts those back to strings, so I can structure the response as nested arrays for batched calls or a single array for individual ones. Writing the batch processing loop...

I realize batching is tricky here because images have different resolutions and text prompts vary in length, so processing them individually is the safer approach for now—it's what similar pipelines do anyway.

Looking at how `ImageToTextPipeline` handles batching, I need to manage both image and text dimensions together. Let me design this to process one image-text pair at a time...

Writing the pipeline loop...

Now I'm figuring out where to place this new class in the file—it should slot in after `ImageToTextPipeline` around line 2005.

For the SUPPORTED_TASKS registration, I'll add it right after the "image-to-text" entry. I'm also deciding whether to use `this.processor` or `this.tokenizer` for decoding the output—since we're generating text tokens, `this.tokenizer.batch_decode()` makes more sense here than the processor.

But I'm realizing a potential issue: if the processor already includes a tokenizer component, loading both a standalone tokenizer and a processor means we'd be loading the tokenizer twice. Let me check how existing multimodal pipelines handle this...

Looking at `image-to-text` and `automatic-speech-recognition`, they both define both a standalone tokenizer and a processor, so this dual-loading pattern is already established. The standalone tokenizer gets used for decoding the output, while the processor's internal tokenizer handles the encoding step. For `ImageTextToTextPipeline`, I'd follow the same approach: the processor handles encoding both images and text input, then the standalone tokenizer decodes the model's output.

For the default model, I'm thinking `HuggingFaceTB/SmolVLM-256M-Instruct` would work well since it's lightweight and likely has ONNX support available. Now I'll put together the complete implementation plan.

1957	 * Image To Text pipeline using a `AutoModelForVision2Seq`. This pipeline predicts a caption for a given image.
1958	 *
1959	 * **Example:** Generate a caption for an image w/ `Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning`.
1960	 * ```javascript
1961	 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning');
1962	 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
1963	 * const output = await captioner(url);
1964	 * // [{ generated_text: 'a cat laying on a couch with another cat' }]
1965	 * ```
1966	 *
1967	 * **Example:** Optical Character Recognition (OCR) w/ `Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten`.
1968	 * ```javascript
1969	 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten');
1970	 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/handwriting.jpg';
1971	 * const output = await captioner(url);
1972	 * // [{ generated_text: 'Mr. Brown commented icily.' }]
1973	 * ```

1975	export class ImageToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
1976	
1977	    /**
1978	     * Create a new ImageToTextPipeline.
1979	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
1980	     */
1981	    constructor(options) {
1982	        super(options);
1983	    }
1984	
1985	    /** @type {ImageToTextPipelineCallback} */
1986	    async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {}) {
1987	
1988	        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
1989	        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);
1990	
1991	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);
1992	
1993	        const toReturn = [];
1994	        for (const batch of pixel_values) {
1995	            batch.dims = [1, ...batch.dims]
1996	            const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: batch, ...generate_kwargs });
1997	            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
1998	                skip_special_tokens: true,
1999	            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }))
2000	            toReturn.push(decoded);
2001	        }
2002	
2003	        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
2004	    }
2005	}

2724	 * ```javascript

2726	 * const image = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/invoice.png';

2729	 * // [{ answer: 'us-001' }]
2730	 * ```

## Plan: Add `image-text-to-text` Pipeline

After thorough codebase exploration, here's the complete implementation plan. **Only `src/pipelines.js` needs to be modified** (4 targeted insertions), plus **1 new test file** to create.

### What was found
- `AutoModelForImageTextToText` already exists in `src/models.js` (line 8736), mapping to 11 model implementations (LLaVA, SmolVLM, Qwen2-VL, PaliGemma, etc.)
- The `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` typedef already covers the needed constructor shape (model + tokenizer + processor)
- The `LlavaProcessor._call(images, text)` pattern returns `{ pixel_values, input_ids, attention_mask }` — confirmed the `{ ...inputs, ...generate_kwargs }` spread pattern works with `model.generate()`

### 5 changes in execution order

1. **Import** — Add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the `models.js` import block (after `AutoModelForImageFeatureExtraction` on line 43)

2. **Class** — Insert `ImageTextToTextPipeline` after `ImageToTextPipeline` (after line 2005). The pipeline loops over each (image, text) pair, calls `this.processor(image, text)` for combined inputs, calls `model.generate({ ...inputs, ...generate_kwargs })`, decodes with `this.tokenizer.batch_decode`, returns `[{ generated_text }]` for single or `[[{ generated_text }], ...]` for batched.

3. **SUPPORTED_TASKS** — Register `"image-text-to-text"` after `"image-to-text"` with `tokenizer: AutoTokenizer`, `model: AutoModelForImageTextToText`, `processor: AutoProcessor`, `type: "multimodal"`, default model `"HuggingFaceTB/SmolVLM-256M-Instruct"`

4. **JSDoc** — Add `"image-text-to-text"` bullet to the `pipeline()` factory function's task list

5. **Test file** — Create `tests/pipelines/test_pipelines_image_text_to_text.js` following the exact same pattern as existing pipeline tests

No existing pipeline classes or their behaviors are touched.

## Summary

**Only 1 source file to modify:** `src/pipelines.js` (4 insertions) + 1 new test file.

### What was found during exploration
- `AutoModelForImageTextToText` already exists in `src/models.js` (line 8736) with mappings for 11 real models: LLaVA, SmolVLM, Qwen2-VL, PaliGemma, Florence2, Idefics3, Moondream1, and more.
- `LlavaProcessor._call(images, text)` confirms how the processor handles both modalities → returns `{ pixel_values, input_ids, attention_mask }`.
- `_prepare_model_inputs` (models.js line 1616) uses `pick(kwargs, forward_params)` so the `{ ...inputs, ...generate_kwargs }` spread pattern safely filters to only model-relevant keys.
- `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` typedef (line 215) already covers the exact shape needed.
- No `hf-internal-testing` tiny model exists yet for `AutoModelForImageTextToText`, so the test uses structural assertions instead of exact output matching.

### The 5 changes

| Step | What | Location anchor |

| 1 | Import `AutoModelForImageTextToText` | After `AutoModelForImageFeatureExtraction,` (line 43) |
| 2 | Insert `ImageTextToTextPipeline` class + JSDoc typedef | After `ImageToTextPipeline` closing `}` (line 2005) |
| 3 | Add `"image-text-to-text"` to `SUPPORTED_TASKS` | After `"image-to-text"` closing `},` |
| 4 | Add bullet to `pipeline()` factory JSDoc | After the `"image-to-text"` bullet |
| 5 | Create `tests/pipelines/test_pipelines_image_text_to_text.js` | New file |

**1. Import** (line 44) — Added `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the `models.js` import block, between `AutoModelForImageFeatureExtraction` and `PreTrainedModel`.

**2. Class + typedef** (lines 2008–2066) — Inserted after the closing `}` of `ImageToTextPipeline` and before `ImageClassificationSingle`:

### `tests/pipelines/test_pipelines_image_text_to_text.js` — new file

Every change is exactly right. Here's a summary of what was done:

---

## Changes Made

### `src/pipelines.js` — 4 targeted insertions

**1. Import** (line 44) — Added `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the `models.js` import block, between `AutoModelForImageFeatureExtraction` and `PreTrainedModel`.

**2. Class + typedef** (lines 2008–2066) — Inserted after the closing `}` of `ImageToTextPipeline` and before `ImageClassificationSingle`:
- `ImageTextToTextSingle` / `ImageTextToTextOutput` output typedefs
- `ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback` callback typedef (accepts `ImagePipelineInputs`, `string|string[]`, optional `GenerationConfig`)
- `ImageTextToTextPipelineType` composite typedef
- `export class ImageTextToTextPipeline` with JSDoc example, constructor, and `_call` implementation

**3. SUPPORTED_TASKS** (lines 3317–3326) — Registered `"image-text-to-text"` immediately after `"image-to-text"` with `tokenizer: AutoTokenizer`, `model: AutoModelForImageTextToText`, `processor: AutoProcessor`, `type: "multimodal"`, default model `"HuggingFaceTB/SmolVLM-256M-Instruct"`.

**4. Factory JSDoc** (line 3499) — Added `` `"image-text-to-text"`: will return a `ImageTextToTextPipeline`. `` to the accepted task list.

### `tests/pipelines/test_pipelines_image_text_to_text.js` — new file
Follows the exact same structure as all 27 existing pipeline tests: `beforeAll` loads the pipeline, instance-check test, `batch_size=1` test, `batch_size>1` test, `afterAll` disposes. Uses structural assertions (`toHaveProperty("generated_text")`) since no tiny deterministic test model is available for `AutoModelForImageTextToText` yet.
